q.en: What are the three main types of North American grasslands, or prairies?
q.zh: 北美草原（prairies）的三种主要类型是什么？

options.en: pampas, steppe, and veld according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 潘帕斯、干草原、VELD（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says: 'There are three main types—tallgrass, mixed-grass, and shortgrass prairies,' differing mainly by the height of the grasses.
explain.zh: 书中写道：「主要有三种类型——高草、混合草和矮草草原」，主要区别在于草的高度。

q.en: About how much of the original tallgrass prairie remains today?
q.zh: 如今原始高草草原大约还剩多少？

explain.en: A 'Do You Know?' box states: 'The tallgrass prairie once covered over 140 million acres... About 10 percent of this area remains.'
explain.zh: 「你知道吗？」框写道：「高草草原曾覆盖超过1.4亿英亩……约10%的区域留存至今。」

q.en: Why can a prairie survive a fire while young trees and shrubs cannot?
q.zh: 为什么草原能在火灾中幸存，而幼树和灌木却不能？

options.en: prairie grasses have deep roots that send up new stems, while fire kills young trees and shrubs
options.zh: 草原草有深根能长出新茎，而火会烧死幼树和灌木

options.en: fire never reaches the prairie according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 火从未到达草原（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book explains prairie grasses have deep roots from which new stems grow, and 'if any young trees or shrubs are growing on the prairie, they will probably be killed by the flames.'
explain.zh: 书中解释草原草有深根可长出新茎，而「若草原上长出幼树或灌木，它们很可能被火焰烧死」。

q.en: Which animal is described as the fastest in North America, able to run about 60 miles (96 km) per hour?
q.zh: 书中描述哪种动物是北美最快的，能以每小时约60英里（96公里）奔跑？

options.en: prairie dog according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 草原犬鼠（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Under 'Pronghorn Antelope' the book says: 'The fastest animal in North America is the pronghorn antelope... The pronghorn can run as fast as 60 miles (96 km) per hour.'
explain.zh: 在「叉角羚」一节，书中写道：「北美最快的动物是叉角羚……叉角羚能以每小时60英里（96公里）的速度奔跑。」

q.en: In the prairie ecosystem, what are 'decomposers'?
q.zh: 在草原生态系统中，「分解者」（decomposers）是什么？

options.en: animals that eat only plants according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 只吃植物的动物（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: organisms like bacteria and fungi that break down dead matter into nutrients
options.zh: 如细菌和真菌般将死物分解为养分的生物

explain.en: The book lists decomposers as one of the six ecosystem parts: 'organisms that break down dead plants and animals into chemical substances in the soil,' including bacteria and fungi such as mushrooms.
explain.zh: 书中将分解者列为生态系统的六个部分之一：「将死去的动植物分解为土壤中化学物质的生物」，包括细菌和蘑菇等真菌。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word 'forage' refer to?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，「forage」一词指什么？

options.en: food such as grass or grain for plant-eating animals
options.zh: 供食草动物食用的草或谷物等食物

options.en: a kind of burrowing rodent according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种穴居啮齿动物（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'forage (n.)' as 'hay, grain, grass, or other food for plant-eating animals.' Sideoats grama and buffalo grass were important forage plants for bison.
explain.zh: 词汇表将「forage（名词）」定义为「干草、谷物、草或其他供食草动物食用的食物」。-sideoats grama和野牛草曾是野牛的重要 forage 植物。

q.en: What creates the 'rain shadow' that makes the shortgrass prairie dry?
q.zh: 是什么造成了使矮草草原干旱的「雨影」（rain shadow）现象？

options.en: heavy snowfall according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 大量降雪（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: warm, dry winds blowing down from the Rocky Mountains
options.zh: 从落基山脉吹下的温暖干燥的风

explain.en: The book says the shortgrass prairie gets little rain because 'the mountains create a rain shadow in this area. This means that warm, dry winds blowing down from the mountains make the area dry.'
explain.zh: 书中说矮草草原降雨少，因为「山脉在该地区形成雨影。这意味着从山上吹下的温暖干燥的风使该地区变干」。

q.en: According to the book, what happened to the number of bison by 1890, and about how many live in protected areas today?
q.zh: 根据本书，到1890年野牛数量发生了什么变化？如今受保护地区大约生活着多少头？

options.en: by 1890 only about 500 remained; today about 200,000 live in protected areas
options.zh: 到1890年仅剩约500头；如今约200,000头生活在保护区

options.en: they always stayed in the millions according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它们一直保持在数百万头（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book states settlers killed bison in great numbers: 'By 1890, only about 500 bison remained,' and 'Today, about 200,000 bison are living in protected areas, such as Yellowstone National Park.'
explain.zh: 书中说定居者大量猎杀野牛：「到1890年，仅剩约500头野牛」，而「如今约200,000头野牛生活在黄石国家公园等保护区」。

q.en: Why does the author emphasize 'Most things cannot survive fire—but a prairie can!'?
q.zh: 作者为什么要强调「大多数东西无法在火中幸存——但草原可以！」？

options.en: To prove prairies should be burned down according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了证明应该把草原烧光（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To show that fire helps the prairie thrive rather than only destroying it
options.zh: 为了说明火有助于草原繁荣，而非只是破坏它

options.en: To explain how to build a house

explain.en: In 'The Importance of Fire' the author wants readers to see that fire actually helps a prairie thrive by letting deep roots regrow and preventing forests, challenging the idea that fire is only destructive.
explain.zh: 在「火的重要性」一节，作者希望读者看到火实际上有助于草原繁荣——深根重新生长、阻止森林形成，从而挑战「火只具破坏性」的成见。

q.en: What is the author's overall purpose in describing prairie preserves and ways readers can volunteer?
q.zh: 作者描写草原保护区以及读者可以参与的志愿活动，其总体目的是什么？

options.en: To argue that all prairies should become farms according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了论证所有草原都应变为农田（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To teach readers about prairie ecosystems and encourage preserving them
options.zh: 为了向读者介绍草原生态系统并鼓励保护它们

explain.en: The 'Preserving the Prairies' section invites readers to volunteer—collecting seeds or counting birds—so they can 'learn about the great grasslands of North America' and help restore them.
explain.zh: 「保护草原」一节邀请读者做志愿者——收集种子或清点鸟类——以便「了解北美大草原」并帮助恢复它们。

prompt.en: If you volunteered at a prairie preserve, which task from the book (collecting seeds, counting birds, or learning plant names) would you choose, and why?
prompt.zh: 如果你在草原保护区做志愿者，你会选择书中提到的哪项任务（收集种子、清点鸟类，还是学习植物名称）？为什么？

en: Refer to a real detail from the book, such as prairie potholes or specific grasses.
Explain how your choice helps preserve the prairie.
zh: 提及书中真实的细节，如草原壶穴或具体草种。
解释你的选择如何有助于保护草原。
